title: ELICIA'S BOOTH
subtitle: Cicadas at Dusk · Vol 5 · Ch II · The High Priestess
notes: Elicia Temple's recording room. A small isolation booth + console room tucked into the south side of the cathedral, though when the door is shut the cathedral isn't here anymore — only the wires, the reels, the visitors who walked in because they had something they couldn't say anywhere else. Twelve spaces; tight, claustrophobic-by-design, every move counts. Three thresholds for the leap.
ambient_pool: the cicadas outside are louder than they should be for the hour.
the reel-to-reel turns. it doesn't catch on anything.
the booth's red record-light is on. you don't remember turning it on.
the cassette wall hums — twelve hundred conversations all at once, none of them audible.
the cathedral pretends, for a minute, that the booth isn't part of it.
elicia leaves her seat. her chair holds the shape.

spaces.label: ENTRY HALL
spaces.flavor: the front door of the booth annex — visitors walk in here.
arrival_lines: the porch light is on. the cicadas pause when you stand here.
the welcome mat says LISTEN in stitched red. you put it down yourself.

spaces.label: BACK DOOR
spaces.flavor: the service exit — opens onto the cathedral's outer cluster.
arrival_lines: the back door is mostly for taking out tapes that won't be played again.
the cathedral is on the other side. it is doing whatever it does without you.

spaces.label: ROOF ACCESS
spaces.flavor: a ladder up to the roof — the cicadas are loudest from up there.
arrival_lines: the ladder rungs ring like a struck chime under your hands.
the cicadas are clearest from here. they sound like one organism rather than thousands.

spaces.label: CONSOLE
spaces.flavor: elicia's chair · the engineer's seat · faders and the master reel.
arrival_lines: the faders are warm. you can feel each visitor's voice in your palm.
the master reel is half-full of tonight's tape, half-empty of tonight's truth.

spaces.label: BOOTH
spaces.flavor: the live room · soundproofed · one chair, one mic, one direction.
arrival_lines: the booth has its own air. older than the cathedral's.
the mic is at mouth height. somebody else's mouth height.

spaces.label: CONTROL GLASS
spaces.flavor: the window between booth and console · you watch them, they don't watch you back.
arrival_lines: the glass has a fingerprint on it from the inside. yours.
the visitor sees their own reflection here before they see you.

spaces.label: TAPE LIBRARY
spaces.flavor: twelve hundred cassettes, alphabetised, each one a sentence from someone else's mouth.
arrival_lines: the library smells like old coffee and electrical tape.
you find a cassette labeled BOOTH 47 · 11:18 PM and don't remember recording it.

spaces.label: PHONE
spaces.flavor: the landline · calls go out, calls come in, neither one is ever a wrong number.
arrival_lines: the phone is unplugged. the line is still warm.
the receiver smells faintly of the person who held it last.

spaces.label: LOUNGE
spaces.flavor: couch, lamp, magazine that's six months old · the room where visitors wait.
arrival_lines: the cushion still has someone else's weight in it.
the lamp's pull-chain has frayed to a single thread.

spaces.label: KITCHENETTE
spaces.flavor: coffee, water, a small kettle that's always almost-boiling.
arrival_lines: the kettle hums in third gear. it's been doing that all night.
two clean mugs on the rack. neither one is yours.

spaces.label: PATCH BAY
spaces.flavor: the routing wall · forty cables, each labeled in elicia's handwriting.
arrival_lines: you trace a cable from the booth to a destination labeled simply 'self.'
two cables are crossed in a way that means the room can hear itself.

spaces.label: VESTIBULE
spaces.flavor: between the lounge and the booth · the moment a visitor decides whether to go in.
arrival_lines: the vestibule is one square of red carpet. some visitors stop here forever.
you stand on the red square. nothing in particular speaks.

master_reel.label: MASTER REEL
master_reel.notes: Elicia's equivalent of the Magician's steamboat. Each RECORD action ticks the reel by 1. At threshold the reel is full — no more recording, only transcribing what's already there. Some scenarios start with progress baked in; some have a loss condition tied to the reel filling before the work is done.

ladder.label: the reel is empty. all of tonight is still ahead.
the first turn of the reel. it is a small turn.
the reel has caught its second wind.
halfway. you can still hear the first conversation under everything that came after.
the reel is making the sound a thing about to run out makes.
the reel has reached the leader-tape. there is no more tonight to record.
